title: Bart The Lover

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy

jokes.text: You said you wanted a world without zinc, Jimmy. Well, now your car has no battery.
jokes.setup: Educational film premise about zinc being important
jokes.punchline: Immediate, practical consequence of no zinc
jokes.explanation: Absurd educational film parody with escalating consequences

jokes.text: Sorry Jimmy. Without zinc for the rotary mechanism there are no telephones.
jokes.setup: Building on the zinc shortage consequences
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation pattern effectively

jokes.text: The firing pin in your gun was made of... yup... zinc.
jokes.explanation: Peak of the escalation with dark comedy twist

jokes.text: Zinc, come back. Zinc! Zinc, zinc! What? Oh, it was all a dream.
jokes.punchline: Classic 'it was all a dream' twist
jokes.explanation: Predictable 'dream' ending but plays into the parody

jokes.text: Gross! He's picking his nose.
jokes.punchline: Students disgusted by something completely unrelated
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between educational content and kids' actual concerns

jokes.text: If anyone wants to learn more about zinc they're welcome to stay. We can talk about anything. I'll do your homework for you.
jokes.setup: Teacher trying to engage students about zinc
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing teacher's desperation

jokes.text: Chef Lonely Heart's soup for one.
jokes.explanation: Efficiently establishes character situation with humor

jokes.text: I haven't seen you since we doubled our prices.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu character moment - honest to a fault

jokes.text: Bingo, bango, sugar in the gas tank. Your ex-husband strikes again.
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from innocent activity to petty revenge

jokes.text: A personal ad? Why not? It might be fun. Kind of a lark. Come on, come on. Answer the phone. I need a man!
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Edna's desperation beneath casual facade

jokes.text: Ow, my eyeball!
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy beat

jokes.text: Kids, this is a yo-yo. Kind of dull, huh? Not much competition for a video game... or is it?
jokes.setup: Yo-yo seeming boring compared to video games
jokes.explanation: Standard corporate presentation parody

jokes.text: She's beautiful.
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's puppy love

jokes.text: These guys must be millionaires. I'll bet they get all kinds of girls.
jokes.punchline: Bart's naive assumptions about yo-yo fame
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid logic about fame and success

jokes.text: I question the value of this assembly. It will be a pleasant memory when they're pumping gas for a living.
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa character moment - precocious cynicism perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Ooh... careful now. That one grazed my ear. Don't move. You could really get hurt.
jokes.punchline: Skinner's nervous reactions to near misses
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner character moment showing his anxiety

jokes.text: Wait. This isn't you. That's the old Sparkle. Get your worthless butts in the van. We got three more schools. Let's go, go, go!
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing the harsh reality behind the performance

jokes.text: That's your trick? No. Here's my trick. Hoowah!
jokes.explanation: Sets up Bart's overconfidence leading to disaster

jokes.text: Can you believe it? Soon, I'll be able to quit my job and live off the boy.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - unrealistic optimism and lazy parenting combined

jokes.text: Name me one person who's gotten rich by doing yo-yo tricks. Donald Trump? No. Arnold Palmer? No. Bill Cosby? No. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Marge challenging Homer's yo-yo fortune idea
jokes.punchline: Homer's failed attempts to name someone, ending in defeat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with satisfying payoff and callback

jokes.text: Did they have yo-yos? No, they did not have yo-yos. Did the Indians have yo-yos? No, they did not have yo-yos!
jokes.setup: Teaching history while frustrated with yo-yo obsession
jokes.punchline: Incorporating yo-yo prohibition into historical facts
jokes.explanation: Good teacher frustration comedy with historical absurdity

jokes.text: Yo.
jokes.punchline: Student's innocent greeting sounds like yo-yo reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - one word perfectly punctures the teacher's prohibition

jokes.text: A little something I call 'Plucking the Pickle.'
jokes.setup: Bart about to show yo-yo trick
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart innuendo humor, well-executed for the character

jokes.text: I didn't do it.
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart catchphrase perfectly timed for maximum effect

jokes.text: Don't worry about your wee fish, lass. They're going to a better place.
jokes.punchline: Willie's grim comfort delivered in his accent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie character moment with dark humor

jokes.text: Here you go... Just kidding. Here you go... Just kidding.
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing teacher's petty revenge

jokes.text: Your ad said you wanted a man. You got yourself a hummm-dinger.
jokes.punchline: Clearly inappropriate match boasting about himself
jokes.explanation: Standard dating disaster comedy

jokes.text: Just because there's a little snow on the roof... I forget how the rest of that goes.
jokes.setup: Older man trying to be charming about his age
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the typical old man cliche

jokes.text: Someone jammed a Malibu Stacy head down here.
jokes.explanation: Good Simpsons brand reference humor

jokes.text: One plus one equals two?
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment showing his academic struggles

jokes.text: My name is... Woodrow.
jokes.setup: Bart writing response to personal ad
jokes.explanation: Good choice showing Bart's attempt at sophistication

jokes.text: I really hate yo-yos.
jokes.punchline: Including his current grievance against his teacher
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Bart's petty motivation while advancing the plot

jokes.text: Marge, you're a tool of the doghouse makers.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting they buy a doghouse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer paranoia comedy with perfect economy

jokes.text: You've been brainwashed by all those doghouse commercials on TV.
jokes.setup: Homer's conspiracy theory about doghouse makers
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the absurd premise

jokes.text: This is a door. He goes through that. This is a roof. And this happy character here is the sun. He shines down on the house.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment showing his simple-minded approach to complex tasks

jokes.text: I'm starting to get the hang of the floor waxer.
jokes.explanation: Good Bart character moment showing his resilience

jokes.text: I'm the 28th president of the United States.
jokes.setup: Reading teacher's letter saying he's not like other men
jokes.punchline: Bart's literal interpretation of Woodrow Wilson reference
jokes.explanation: Clever kid logic interpreting the name choice historically

jokes.text: You've got a date with the Xerox machine.
jokes.punchline: Bart's plan to copy the photo
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's mischievous problem-solving

jokes.text: Stupid lumber. Damn it! D'oh! I-- Oh, the hell with this!
jokes.punchline: Rapid escalation of frustration and swearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer frustration escalation with signature catchphrase

jokes.text: Hell no. I don't want any damn vegetables.
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly harsh profanity from sweet kid
jokes.explanation: Shocking character break perfectly executed for maximum impact

jokes.text: Was it asparagus?
jokes.setup: Ned calling about son's bad language
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy focusing on wrong aspect of the problem
jokes.explanation: Good Lovejoy character moment showing his disengagement

jokes.text: Uh... page 900.
jokes.setup: Asked where in Bible to direct profanity issue
jokes.punchline: Obviously making up a random page number
jokes.explanation: Shows Lovejoy's lazy approach to pastoral duties

jokes.text: Damn Flanders.
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - swearing right after discussing swearing problem

jokes.text: That exchange student Mix!pa?
jokes.punchline: Foreign exchange student with unusual name
jokes.explanation: Decent throwaway character reference

jokes.text: He used to watch Davy and Goliath but he thought the idea of a talking dog was blasphemous.
jokes.explanation: Excellent Flanders family characterization - absurdly strict religious standards

jokes.text: Maybe it's the beer talking but you've got a butt that won't quit. They got these big, chewy pretzels here... Five dollars?! Get outta here.
jokes.setup: Homer's romantic love letter to Marge
jokes.punchline: Stream of consciousness from brewery to complaint about prices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer 'romantic' writing - combines genuine affection with typical Homer distractions

jokes.text: Wow, a side of Dad I've never seen!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sarcastic reaction to Homer's 'romantic' side
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa sarcasm in response to Homer's 'romance'

jokes.text: Truly, yours is a butt that won't quit.
jokes.setup: Bart writing romantic letter to teacher
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Bart borrowing Homer's 'romantic' language

jokes.text: What the hell are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Flanders complaining about Homer's potty mouth
jokes.punchline: Homer swearing while denying he swears
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment - swearing while being accused of swearing

jokes.text: All of us pull a few boners now and then-- Go off half-cocked, make asses of ourselves.
jokes.setup: Ned trying to be diplomatic about Homer's swearing
jokes.punchline: Ned accidentally using innuendo while trying to be proper
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ned character moment - accidentally crude while trying to be proper

jokes.text: Oh, come on now, Flanders. I don't complain about your... moustache.
jokes.explanation: Good Homer deflection tactic showing his argumentative style

jokes.text: Uh, it makes you look like you got something to hide. People are talking. Lots of people.
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's petty attack

jokes.text: Aye, aye... Admiral Butthead.
jokes.setup: Making deal with Ned about behavior changes
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer immaturity in the face of adult agreement

jokes.text: Whoa, slow down, Frenchy. This stuff is gold.
jokes.punchline: Bart recognizing material he can steal for letters
jokes.explanation: Great Bart opportunism moment - always looking for an angle

jokes.text: Homer, you brought up Ned Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about Marge bringing up Flanders
jokes.explanation: Good Marge logic moment deflating Homer's complaint

jokes.text: It almost cost him his job as a baby photographer
jokes.setup: Marge's father used to swear a lot
jokes.punchline: Specific inappropriate job for someone with profanity problem
jokes.explanation: Excellent specificity - swearing around babies is perfectly inappropriate

jokes.text: If I catch fire? If I see something weird? What about when we snuggle?
jokes.setup: Homer asking about swear jar exceptions
jokes.punchline: Progression from reasonable to absurd scenarios
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer trying to find loopholes

jokes.text: Ay, caramba!
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of Bart's catchphrase for comic timing

jokes.text: Perhaps later we will smooch up a storm. Sexily yours, Woody.
jokes.punchline: Kid's awkward attempt at adult romance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from Bart's innocent inappropriateness

jokes.text: Homer, that was a 20.
jokes.setup: Homer putting money in swear jar
jokes.punchline: Homer's excessive payment for single swear
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the swear jar concept

jokes.text: No sooner had I shaved off the old cookie duster than a lady cast me in a commercial.
jokes.setup: Ned thanking Homer for moustache advice
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff to the moustache subplot

jokes.text: Well, he just goes... Ohh!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how dog enters doghouse
jokes.punchline: Homer getting stuck in his own badly designed door
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff to Homer's construction incompetence

jokes.text: What about Principal Skinner? Let's just say his mommy won't let him out to play.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character reference with efficient dismissal

jokes.text: What about Coach Fortner? Glug, glug, glug, glug...
jokes.explanation: Efficient character dismissal with sound effect economy

jokes.text: What about groundskeeper Willy? Ugh! I won't even tell you what that guy's into.
jokes.punchline: Vague but ominous rejection of Willie
jokes.explanation: Good Willie character reference with mysterious dark humor

jokes.text: Bart, you are the closest thing to a man in my life and that's so depressing I think I'm going to cry.
jokes.punchline: Realizing a 10-year-old is her best male relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Edna's desperation and the irony of her situation

jokes.text: Oh, fudge... That's broken. Fiddle-dee-dee. That will require a tetanus shot.
jokes.setup: Homer trying not to swear while injured
jokes.punchline: Absurdly mild substitutes for increasingly serious injuries
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Homer's attempt to avoid swearing despite increasing pain

jokes.text: Beer!
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - simple pleasure in beer discovery

jokes.text: Dear baby, Welcome to Dumpville. Population: You.
jokes.setup: Homer offering to write sensitive breakup letter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - completely missing the point of 'sensitive' with memorable cruelty

jokes.text: Though I'll be inoculating babies in Kampuchea my heart is with you.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - precocious vocabulary for romantic excuse

jokes.text: How about 'Crocodiles bit off my face'?
jokes.explanation: Good Bart character moment - casual violence as excuse

jokes.text: Three simple words: 'I am gay.'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer math incompetence combined with his go-to excuse solution

jokes.text: Homer, you old honey dripper.
jokes.setup: Homer contributing romantic language to letter
jokes.punchline: Marge's affectionate response to Homer's rare sweetness
jokes.explanation: Nice Marge reaction to Homer's unexpected romantic contribution

jokes.text: It's a date.
jokes.explanation: Perfect button ending with romantic irony between teacher and student

jokes.text: It's such a nice day today. Let's have detention outside. It's a date.
jokes.explanation: Nice resolution showing improved teacher-student relationship

tmdb_overview: Bart's answer to detention from Mrs. Krabappel is to answer her personal ad with letters from a Mr. Wonderful, but his last laugh is more of a guilty whimper.

guest_stars.name: Pamela Hayden
guest_stars.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Rod Flanders / Sparkle (voice)

guest_stars.name: Maggie Roswell
guest_stars.character: Miss Hoover / Maude Flanders / Helen Lovejoy (voice)

insight: Escalation-driven character comedy sustains 76 jokes across 22 minutes at peak Season 3 form.
